title: [Bug]: NATS seed URLs accept unsupported schemes
summary: The optional NATS seed URL list validates only blank values and control characters, allowing unsupported schemes to remain in configuration.
observed: A multi-server configuration can include an unsupported seed URL scheme and still pass validation.
expected: Every configured NATS seed URL must use an explicitly supported NATS client scheme.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that places an unsupported scheme in the NATS seed URL list.
impact: Operators can believe failover seed URLs are valid while the client receives an unsafe or unusable connection target.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. Invalid connection configuration fails before message consumption begins.
security: The fix applies allow-list validation at the network configuration boundary.
acceptance: Unsupported seed URL schemes are rejected during configuration validation.
Supported seed URL lists remain accepted.
The rejection identifies the seed URL list field.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, NATS connection option tests, configuration tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update configuration and NATS connection documentation if accepted schemes are clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
